findings.claim_under_test: A label-adaptive balanced coordinate layout obtains polynomial fiber bond from 2-adic valuation grouping or finite exact-rank optimization.
findings.evidence: Adaptive valuation theorems=1, inverse-polynomial no-go rows=5, exact optimum rows/layout evaluations=3/8837, maximum finite improvement bits=1.4739311883324122, fitted best-rank slope=0.4687412361808221, polynomial selector/contractions=0, general adaptive no-go theorems=0, relation solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Reject valuation-only layout compression and any selector using exact 2^q residue scores. A surviving additive layout must have a polynomial selector, an all-n polynomial-bond and source-coverage theorem, matched classical contraction, and verified relation output.

findings.claim_under_test: Escalating to a sufficiently large but fixed source factorial-moment order yields persistent generic density-one modular subset-sum signal.
findings.evidence: Instantiated/proved fixed-order certificates=11/11; general all-fixed theorem=1; growing-order obstructions=0; atypical-fiber obstructions=0.
findings.required_action: Stop increasing fixed moment degree. Specify k(n) and charge its transfer states, records, memory, and decoder cost; alternatively prove an inverse-polynomial atypical conditioned-fiber tail or use a non-moment reduced-basis mechanism.

findings.claim_under_test: Exact fixed-depth coverage over every legal target for finitely many public-label rows proves an asymptotic success or failure law under random labels.
findings.evidence: All-target censuses/max n/depth=12/20/2; assignments/legal targets=16711680/4081274; tail standard/carry coverage=0.9039562807239387/0.9516438429432461; kernel/full-cube failures=0/0; asymptotic bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Use exact target coverage only to select a label-dependent reduced-basis statistic. Prove its random-label concentration and implication for fixed-depth coverage, or abandon this branching grammar; do not call finite all-target completeness an asymptotic affine-CVP result.

findings.claim_under_test: Exponential separation of nearby Boolean witnesses in a uniform legal density-one subset-sum coset already supplies, or implies, an efficient marker-aware affine decoder.
findings.evidence: Uniform-legal/fixed-beta separation theorems=1/1; exact source-census failures=0; tail inverse-polynomial close-pair no-go rows=6; marker-aware decoders/coverage proofs=0/0.
findings.required_action: Use the separation theorem only as source-correct geometry. Construct a polynomial-time marker-aware decoder that returns a verified Boolean witness, prove inverse-polynomial coverage under the same independent-uniform-target-conditioned-legal source, and account for far witnesses and reduced-basis distortion before promoting the route.

findings.claim_under_test: A bounded-locality Pauli measurement efficiently exposes the global DCP contamination signal.
findings.evidence: All 4 locality certificates make the aggregate signed-relation probability negligible; finite trials found 11 rare relations but 0 polynomial robust witnesses.
findings.required_action: Move beyond bounded-locality Pauli correlators to an implicit global measurement with polynomial circuit complexity, then prove adversarial robustness and full-decoder composition.

findings.claim_under_test: Choosing a reachable low carry creates a specially distributed ordinary high quotient, or trying all carries converts an exponentially rare generic high-only event into inverse-polynomial coverage.
findings.evidence: Conditional product/low-selector/union-bound theorem counts=1/1/1; exact translation control failures=0; joint low/high no-go theorems=0.
findings.required_action: Reject high-only distribution-bias claims after low carry selection. A surviving preconditioner must retain joint low/high constraints, prove carry-restricted witness geometry, or establish that a concrete generic quotient event already has inverse-polynomial probability.

findings.claim_under_test: Polynomial enumeration of exact low-bit carry slices converts finite LLL recovery into a Regev-compatible partial average-case subset-sum solver.
findings.evidence: Paired baseline/sliced successes=30/31, sliced-only/baseline-only=2/1, tail baseline/sliced=8/7, and uniform coverage proofs=0.
findings.required_action: Retain the carry-sliced embedding as a deterministic polynomial solver class, but require a uniform average-case short-vector separation and legal-coverage theorem. Reject scale tuning or finite paired success as evidence of exponent collapse.

findings.claim_under_test: Constant clean covariant-PGM success at m=Theta(n) supplies an efficient DCP decoder.
findings.evidence: Mean exact m=n PGM success=0.5826527495468754, clean information theorems=1, polynomial PGM circuits=0, coherent fiber erasures=0, exact-f=1 robust PGMs=0.
findings.required_action: Keep the positive clean information theorem, but block algorithmic claims until a uniform poly(n) normalized-fiber isometry, Gram block encoding, or collision walk is constructed without N-sized advice, then prove exact f=1 robustness and lattice composition.

findings.claim_under_test: Finite Clifford Hamming bias is robust to the f=1 arbitrary bad-register promise.
findings.evidence: Across 2808 fixed one-bad cases, the maximum robust Hamming TV was 0.03125 with fitted log2 slope -0.7235643472030779; the audit proved 0 full thresholds and 0 decoders.
findings.required_action: Find a uniform efficient statistic with nondecaying worst-case bias, extend it beyond one bad register to the full f=1 promise, and compose it with hidden-reflection recovery.

findings.claim_under_test: Global Clifford output nonuniformity gives an efficiently decoded robust DCP signal.
findings.evidence: The exact sweep saw maximum unrestricted TV 0.162109375 but maximum Hamming-decoded TV 0.01904296875, fitted log2 Hamming-TV slope -0.6287216445695861, and 0 proved signal families.
findings.required_action: Derive a uniform inverse-polynomial statistic with polynomial decision cost, then prove partial-contamination robustness and full hidden-reflection decoding.

findings.claim_under_test: A polynomial compute-subset-sum/QFT circuit reveals the hidden DCP reflection.
findings.evidence: Finite instances=20, QFT uniformity failures=0, compute/QFT signal instances=0. The output is proved exactly uniform while orthogonal input garbage remains.
findings.required_action: Reject compute-sum/QFT architectures without coherent fiber symmetrization. Require an explicit interference mechanism and polynomial circuit rather than a sum ancilla alone.

findings.claim_under_test: Exact polynomial-bond tensor networks can prepare density-one random linear-depth subset-sum fiber states and thereby supply the missing child-fiber relation mechanism.
findings.evidence: Exact Schmidt theorems=1, random exponential-rank theorems=1, exact density-one bond no-go theorems=1, minimum certified hard instance probability=0.2857142857142857, approximate bond/layout-dictionary/general-circuit no-go theorems=1/1/0, polynomial state preparations/solvers=0/0.
findings.required_action: Reject exact and 99-percent-fidelity low-bond density-one fiber preparation, including fixed polynomial layout dictionaries. A surviving partial-instance or fully label-adaptive layout tensor route must prove source-law coverage, polynomial coherent contraction or preparation, verified relation output, and a matched classical tensor baseline.

findings.claim_under_test: An exact polynomial-bond residue tensor network realizes collision-block interference.
findings.evidence: Bond certificates=6, high-probability exponential-bond certificates=4, polynomial collective measurements=0.
findings.required_action: Reject exact residue-state MPS/DP implementations. Search approximate hashed networks or compressed fiber measurements, preserving the restricted scope of the bond theorem.

findings.claim_under_test: Polynomially many explicit signed-difference masks can realize the large set-theoretic child-fiber pairing mass seen at linear depth.
findings.evidence: Linear minimum-support theorems=1, dictionary coverage theorems=1, union-bound exponent=-0.10020993338298612, existence no-go rows=3, dictionary coverage no-go rows=1, implicit target-indexed no-go theorems=0, and polynomial solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Reject polynomial explicit mask dictionaries. A surviving target-dependent partial map must be implicitly indexed or nontranslation, and must prove source-law coverage, polynomial construction, verified output, and a classical separation under the same relation access.

findings.claim_under_test: Either f=1 contamination destroys global PGM information, or proving robust information supplies an efficient decoder.
findings.evidence: All-good lower-bound violations=0, exact-f=1 information robustness proofs=1, polynomial robust PGM circuits=0, lattice compositions=0.
findings.required_action: Use the proved product-mixture all-good bound as the robustness invariant. Stop proposing signal-only f=1 witnesses; construct the full-rank measurement circuit and compose its complete output with the exact reduction.

findings.claim_under_test: Finite cross-child connectivity or positive transport-graph spectral gaps establish a polynomial quantum walk relation solver.
findings.evidence: Linear-depth rows=16, fragmented rows=16, zero-cross-child rows=9, minimum positive finite gap=2.220446049250313e-16, maximum classical BFS visits=529, uniform gap theorems=0, polynomial walks=0, and classical separations=0.
findings.required_action: Prove uniform random-fiber conductance, polynomial linear-depth state preparation and coherent updates, verified relation output, and an asymptotic advantage over classical access to the same graph.

findings.claim_under_test: Finite growth or one-step-tree escape of exact marker-witness rounding deviations establishes a lower bound against all polynomial marker-aware affine decoders.
findings.evidence: Complete legal profiles/replay failures/max n=19/0/36; tail depth-two standard/carry=0/0 over 3; tree escapes=0/0; asymptotic growth theorems=0.
findings.required_action: Use exact profiles to formulate and prove an LLL-coordinate source law for a specific branching grammar, or design a decoder outside that grammar. Do not transfer finite one-step escape to growing offsets, different bases, enumeration, walks, or general affine-CVP complexity.

findings.claim_under_test: An inverse-polynomial source mass of low-bit fibers retains inverse-polynomial conditional signal from a nonnegative source-nongeneric fixed-order tuple contribution.
findings.evidence: Conditioned tail certificates=33/33; general fixed-order tail theorem=1; growing/signed/basis tail proofs=0/0/0.
findings.required_action: Discard selected fixed-order energetic fibers. A surviving route must charge growing order, prove a signed observable is not dominated by bad tuples without exponential variance, or state an explicit reduced-basis event with inverse-polynomial coverage and decoder implication.

findings.claim_under_test: Random power-of-two subset-sum carries admit a uniformly bounded-degree algebraic solver.
findings.evidence: Carry rows=144, tail bounded-degree rows=0/48, maximum ANF degree=16, final-bit degree slope=1.075, polynomial algebraic solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Reject bounded-degree carry reconstruction for the tested random-label family. Require a uniform symbolic exception and polynomial witness solver before reopening it; preserve that finite high ANF degree is not a lower bound against lattice, representation, quantum, or other non-algebraic routes.

findings.claim_under_test: Local Hadamard measurement below the critical state ratio has inverse-polynomial decoding signal.
findings.evidence: The signed-relation second-moment bound places 12 rows below alpha=1.7095112913514547; supercritical finite rows give 0 worst-reflection proofs and 0 robust decoders.
findings.required_action: Abandon subcritical Hadamard decoder tuning; for supercritical ratios prove worst-reflection signal, polynomial repetition cost, exact f=1 robustness, and full decoding.

findings.claim_under_test: Polynomial subset-sum hashing makes uniform coherent fiber erasure efficient.
findings.evidence: Finite instances=32, hidden-average identity failures=0, high-probability worst-d no-go certificates=5, polynomial fiber symmetrizations=0.
findings.required_action: Reject uniform Hadamard erasure and amplitude amplification of its postselection. Apply the public low-trace reference theorem before considering full-rank measurements or adaptive collision walks.

findings.claim_under_test: A biased or smooth uniformly margin-separated one-pass linear score gives a joint-polynomial DCP frequency localizer.
findings.evidence: Margin-energy certificates=24, finite optimality failures=0, joint-polynomial rows=0, arbitrary linear-classifier lower bounds=0, nonlinear lower bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Exclude uniformly margin-separated one-score MSE-certified linear bucket mutations. Search adaptive multiple scores or nonlinear record coupling, and do not generalize this restricted theorem.

findings.claim_under_test: A fixed-degree product kernel on disjoint iid DCP records gives joint-polynomial coarse-frequency localization.
findings.evidence: Degree-indexed certificates=120, finite failures=0, higher-degree improvements=0, joint-polynomial rows=0, overlapping U-statistic lower bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Exclude disjoint fixed-degree product kernels. Analyze overlapping degenerate U-statistics, adaptive score families, implicit contractions, or collective measurements without extending this theorem to them.

findings.claim_under_test: Explicit all-subsets overlapping product U-statistics give a joint-polynomial DCP frequency localizer.
findings.evidence: Hoeffding certificates=140, coefficient failures=0, joint-polynomial explicit rows=0, polynomial-record/exponential-tuple rows=59, implicit-contraction lower bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Exclude explicit all-subsets product kernels. Require a polynomial implicit contraction with audited intermediate dimension and precision, or move to a non-product/collective estimator class.

findings.claim_under_test: Exact unbiased linear iid hash bins give a joint-polynomial DCP frequency localizer.
findings.evidence: Parseval certificates=24, finite transform failures=0, joint-polynomial rows=0, nonlinear lower bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Exclude exact linear bucket indicators from mutation; search biased estimators with proved margins or genuinely nonlinear sketches, while preserving the restriction of this no-go.

findings.claim_under_test: A rank-one elementary-symmetric implicit contraction yields a joint-polynomial DCP bucket decoder.
findings.evidence: Rank-one certificates=120, finite variance failures=0, joint-polynomial rows=0, polynomial-rank lower bounds=0, tensor-train lower bounds=0.
findings.required_action: Exclude scalar rank-one power kernels. Require explicit polynomial-rank projection cancellation or a low-bond tensor contraction with coefficient norm, precision, and intermediate dimension charged.

findings.claim_under_test: Polynomial-rank Fejer/Fourier contractions provide a joint-polynomial random-label DCP decoder.
findings.evidence: Rows=24, uniform separators=16, superpolynomial-sample rows=16, finite joint-polynomial survivors=0, proved uniform families=0.
findings.required_action: Treat tested cosine/Fejer/hybrid dictionaries as negative evidence. Require a new projection-cancellation mechanism and retain exact all-order covariance, precision, contraction, f=1, and lattice gates.

findings.claim_under_test: A known subset-sum exponent improvement or basic generalized-birthday tree satisfies Regev's polynomial partial-solver contract.
findings.evidence: Known algorithms=7, polynomial algorithms=0, best classical/quantum exponents=0.255/0.218, deep Wagner threshold failures=36/36, Regev-contract solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Reject positive exponential exponents as solutions to a polynomial contract. Require new density-one structure, inverse-polynomial legal coverage, and a deterministic or coherently proved replacement interface; preserve that the Wagner threshold is a class-specific random-list audit, not a lower bound.

findings.claim_under_test: O(log n) low-bit conditioning creates a lower-density high-quotient search whose exact or near-residual candidate counts explain a polynomial partial subset-sum solver.
findings.evidence: Exact conditional first/second-factorial/variance certificates=10/10/10; maximum density exponent change=0.0; LLL geometry theorems=0; polynomial witness solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Reject count-only and fixed residual-window mechanisms. Reopen the preconditioner only with a proved higher-order correlation, changed LLL basis geometry, or implicit decoder that has inverse-polynomial legal coverage; pairwise independence is not a general subset-sum lower bound.

findings.claim_under_test: A fixed residual statistic of order at most four generically reveals a logarithmically conditioned density-one subset-sum witness.
findings.evidence: Triplewise independence/fourth-localization certificates=8/8; tail maximum additive-energy inflation=2.5837260924233405; tail relative fourth-excess upper bound=0.039362420761545086; asymptotic fourth-order obstructions=8; witness solvers=0.
findings.required_action: Discard all degree<=3 residual statistics, affine-independent fourth tuples, and generic source-average fixed-fourth excess. Reopen order four only with an inverse-polynomial atypical-fiber tail plus an implicit decoder. Otherwise move explicitly to growing-order or reduced-basis structure; finite Walsh tables are not algorithms or lower bounds.

findings.claim_under_test: A marker-aware affine mechanism has evidence of quantum advantage before surviving polynomial target-dependent nearest-plane lists around the standard and carry-sliced Babai cells.
findings.evidence: Fixed-depth polynomial-list theorems/count failures/max depth=1/0/2; depth-zero/max-depth standard successes=0/0, carry successes=0/1; invalid outputs=0; source-coverage theorems=0; tail standard/carry/legals=0/0/4.
findings.required_action: Treat every fixed-depth success as a legal classical attack. Derive the uniform-legal source mass of the implemented cell union or show its collapse asymptotically; failure at depths one and two closes only those polynomial lists and cannot support a general affine-CVP lower bound.

findings.claim_under_test: The current DCP workbench improves a named legal decoder resource frontier.

findings.claim_under_test: Multiplicative-character hidden shifts support a large query-complexity separation.
findings.evidence: Character query-information audit found 8 row(s) with logarithmic random-sample query ceilings by pairwise agreement; max union-bound query count=15, max q/log2(p)=2.50.
findings.required_action: Do not pursue this family as a query lower-bound candidate; state the remaining claim as a computational decoding-time lower bound and compare against explicit classical decoders.

findings.claim_under_test: The unresolved uniform shifted-character decoder gap is evidence for a Shor-level route.
findings.evidence: No uniform polylogarithmic classical decoder is recorded, but there is also no unconditional superpolynomial decoding lower bound and no model-preserving reduction to a major natural problem.
findings.required_action: Attach a natural reduction or named hardness assumption with explicit average/worst-case and preprocessing semantics; otherwise treat this as an oracle separation and retire it from the top frontier.

findings.claim_under_test: Shifted multiplicative characters provide a classical-query or unconditional online-time separation.
findings.evidence: The complexity ledger records 12 literature-backed logarithmic-query/domain-time upper-bound row(s) and 12 fixed-prefix online decode(s) after domain-size preprocessing/advice.
findings.required_action: Disallow query-advantage language. State a uniform single-instance no-preprocessing model and count all modulus-dependent advice and repeated-instance amortization.

findings.claim_under_test: Multiplicative-character hidden shifts provide speedup evidence before a decoding lower bound is proved.
findings.evidence: Character lower-bound ledger has 22 random-sample fingerprint row(s), 28 chosen-query fingerprint row(s), 23 pair-ratio candidate-filter recovery row(s), and 26 full-degree cyclotomic GCD recovery row(s).
findings.required_action: State and prove a poly(log p) decoding lower bound, or find a polynomial-style classical decoder; do not promote query-efficient fingerprints as quantum evidence.

findings.claim_under_test: Hidden-shift sampled-query survival supports a quantum speedup claim.
findings.evidence: Query lower-bound probe found 132 row(s) where polynomially many samples fingerprint the shift if exhaustive candidate enumeration is allowed and 228 row(s) with logarithmic pairwise-agreement query ceilings.
findings.required_action: State the remaining claim as a decoding-time lower bound, then either prove it or search for a non-exhaustive classical decoder.
